title: festuca ovina subsp. brevifolia
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. brevifolia, also known as blue sheep's fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of the world, particularly in the Himalayas and parts of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The blue sheep's fescue can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits.
